question.text:
In the context of compilers, which of the following 
is/are NOT an intermediate representation of the 
source program?

solution.text:
In the context of compilers, symbol table is not an 
intermediate representation of the source program. 
Symbol table is a table used to store attributes 
information and it is used in all the phases of 
compilers. Three address code, AST, Control Flow 
Graph, and DAG are intermediate representations.

question.text:
Consider the expression (a-1)*(((b+c)/3)+d).  

Let X be the minimum number of registers required 
by an optimal code generation (without any register 
spill) algorithm for a load/store architecture, in 
which  

(i)  only load and store instructions can have 
memory operands and  

(ii)  arithmetic instructions can have only register or 
immediate operands.  

The value of X is______.

solution.text:
X is minimum number of registers without any 
register spill.  

So, 


R1 ← b 


R2 ← c  


R1 ← R1 + R2 


R1 ← R1 /3 


R2 ← d 


R1 ← R1 + R2 


R2 ← a 


R2 ← R2 - 1 


R1 ← R2 \times R1 

Memory operands a, b, c, and d are used only with 
load/store instructions. In all arithmetic instructions, 
we have used only register or immediate 
operand(constant). Registers R1 and R2 are enough 
to covert given expression into assembly code 
without any additional registers. So, Minimum 
number of registers required is 2.

question.text:
Consider the following intermediate program in 
three address code 


p = a - b  


q = p * c 


p = u * v 


q = p + q 

Which one of the following corresponds to a static 
single assignment form of the above code?

options.text:
p3 = a − b   


q1 = p1∗c 

q4 = p3∗c 

p1 = u ∗ v 

p4 = u∗v 

q1 = p1 + q1 

q5 = p4 + q4

p1 = a−b 


q1 = p2∗c 

q1 = p ∗ c 

p3 = u∗v 

q2 = u ∗ v 

q2− p4 + q3 

q2 = p + q

solution.text:
The corresponding single static assignment is  

p3 = a - b 

q4 = p3∗c 

p4 = u∗v 

q5 = p4 + q4 


All of a, b, c, u, v, p3, p4, q4, and q5 have single static 
assignment.

y = x ∗ y 

= (y + w) ∗ (t - z) 

= ([x ∗ v] + w) ∗ (t - z) 

y = ([u - t] ∗ v + w) ∗ (t - z)  

u1 = u - t 

u2 = u1 - v 

u3 = u2 - w 

t1 = t - z 

u4 = u3 - t1 

Total variables, u, v, t, w, z, u1, u2, u3, u4, t1. 


So, total 10 variables are required for static single 
assignment form.

question.text:
For a C program accessing 𝑋[𝑖][𝑗][𝑘], the following 
intermediate code is generated by a compiler. 
Assume that the size of an integer is 32 bits and the 
size of a character is 8 bits. 



t0 = i∗1024  


t1 = j∗32  


t2 = k∗4 


t3 = t1+t0 


t4 = t3+t2 


t5 = X[t4] 

Which one of the following statements about the 
source code for the C program is CORRECT?

options.text:
X is declared as "char X[32][16][2]". 
Three Address Code

solution.text:
t5  
= X[t4] 


= X [t3 + t2] 


= X [t1 + t0 + k \times 4] 


= X[j \times 32 + i \times 1024 + k \times 4] 

t5 = X [i \times 1024 + j \times 32 + k \times 4] 
         Note: & X[i][j][[k] = &  X[0][0][0] + (i×qr + j×r + 
k) \times size of elements 

t5  
= X [i \times 256 + j \times 8 + k] \times 4 =  X [i \times 32 \times 8 
+ j \times 8 + k] \times 4 

Comparing it with options, we can say X is declared 
as "int X[32][32][8]"". Therefore, option A is the 
correct answer.

question.text:
The least number of temporary variables required to 
create a three-address code in static single 
assignment form for the expression  

q+r/3+s−t∗5+u∗v/w is _______.

solution.text:
For the given expression:  q+r/3+s−t∗5+u∗v/w, the 
corresponding three address code is:  



Now, this optimized three address code have 7 
variables (r, t, u, v, w, q, s). 

To produce the SSA form, we will get one new 
temporary variable in each instruction. 

SSA code will have 15 total variables (r, t, u, v, w, 
q, s, r1, t1, u1, u2, q1, q2, q3, q4). So, we have 7 
variables already defined outside expression and 8 
temporary variables used in the expression.
